profileStorage:
Profiles are isolated HERMES_HOME directories on disk. The "default" profile is the canonical install at `~/.hermes/` itself (zero-migration compat path). Named profiles live under `~/.hermes/profiles/<id>/` (each one a full self-contained Hermes root with its own config.yaml, .env, memories/, sessions/, skills/, gateway/, cron/, logs/, skins/, plans/, workspace/, home/, SOUL.md, and optional distribution.yaml).

Per-profile metadata (description + description_auto flag, used by the kanban decomposer for role-based routing) is persisted in `<profile_dir>/profile.yaml` — a small YAML file separate from the ~5000-line config.yaml. The bundled-skill-seed opt-out marker `.no-bundled-skills` lives in the profile root.

The sticky active-profile pointer is a single-line text file at `~/.hermes/active_profile` (empty/missing => default).

Wrapper alias scripts for each profile live at `~/.local/bin/<id>` (POSIX shell script) or `~/.local/bin/<id>.bat` (Windows); they simply `exec hermes -p <id> "$@"`.

Config-storage format is YAML for `config.yaml` / `profile.yaml` / `distribution.yaml`; JSON for `auth.json`, `gateway_state.json`, `honcho.json`, `processes.json`, `channel_directory.json`. Skill enable/disable state is stored under the `skills:` key in each profile's `config.yaml` (NOT a separate file):

  skills:
    disabled: [skill-a, skill-b]          # global disabled list (list of skill names)
    platform_disabled:                    # optional per-platform overrides
      telegram: [skill-c]
      cli: []

Format is JSON for `~/.skills_prompt_snapshot.json` (the prompt-builder cache) and a lockfile in the hub dir. Skills themselves are plain directories under `<profile_dir>/skills/<category>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` (markdown with YAML frontmatter). Installed hub skills are tracked in a lockfile; bundled skills in `~/.hermes/skills/.bundled_manifest` (read via `tools/skills_sync._read_manifest`).
defaultProfileMechanism:
The bare `hermes` command (no `-p` flag) is resolved in this precedence order at process start, in `_apply_profile_override()` in `hermes_cli/main.py` (runs BEFORE any hermes module import so HERMES_HOME is correct for the entire process):

1. Explicit `--profile` / `-p` flag (anywhere in argv) or `--profile=<name>` form, e.g. `hermes -p coder chat`, `hermes chat -p coder`, or `hermes -pcoder`. Validated against `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$` early to reject pytest-style misreads.
2. `HERMES_HOME` env var — but only if its parent directory is named `profiles` (i.e. it already points to a specific profile dir like `~/.hermes/profiles/coder`). If HERMES_HOME is the hermes root, step 3 applies.
3. Sticky `~/.hermes/active_profile` file — single-line name; "default" (or missing/empty) resolves to `~/.hermes` itself; any other name resolves to `~/.hermes/profiles/<name>` via `resolve_profile_env()`. SUPERVISED S6 gateway children skip this step (reserved `gateway-default` slot must NOT follow sticky default).
4. Final fallback: platform-native `~/.hermes` (via `hermes_constants._get_platform_default_hermes_home()`).

`hermes_cli/profiles.resolve_profile_env(name)` is the canonical name->path resolver. `get_active_profile_name()` infers the *current* profile from HERMES_HOME (returns "default" for the root, the profile id for `~/.hermes/profiles/<id>`, or "custom" for an unrecognized path).

Set the sticky default with `hermes profile use <name>` (writes `active_profile`; removes the file when target is "default").
skillEnableDisableMechanism:
Hermes uses a NEGATIVE-LIST (allowlist-by-omission) model. There is no per-skill `enable_skill` / `disable_skill` boolean flag and no `skills_enabled` set. The mechanism is:

- `config["skills"]["disabled"]: List[str]` — global list of skill names that are OFF for every platform.
- `config["skills"]["platform_disabled"][<platform>]: List[str]` — per-platform additive overrides (unioned with the global list, never replacing it).
- A skill is "enabled" iff its name is NOT in the union of the global list and the active platform's list.

Runtime resolution:
- `agent/skill_utils.py:get_disabled_skill_names(platform=None)` — single source of truth at runtime. Reads `~/.hermes/config.yaml` directly (no CLI import). Platform is resolved from explicit arg, then `HERMES_PLATFORM` env, then `HERMES_SESSION_PLATFORM` session env.
- `hermes_cli/skills_config.py:get_disabled_skills(config, platform)` and `save_disabled_skills(config, disabled, platform)` — TUI-facing mutators that call `load_config`/`save_config` and write back via `save_config`.
- `hermes_cli/skills_hub.py:do_list(..., enabled_only=False)` annotates each skill with enabled/disabled status from the resolved list.

Available CLI verbs (registered in `hermes_cli/subcommands/skills.py`, dispatched in `hermes_cli/skills_hub.py:skills_command`):
- `hermes skills config` — interactive curses UI that toggles individual skills or whole categories (writes `skills.disabled` / `skills.platform_disabled`).
- `hermes skills list` — lists installed skills with status (reads disabled list to annotate).
- `hermes skills install <identifier>` — installs a skill by `openai/skills/skill-creator` style identifier or direct URL.
- `hermes skills uninstall <name>` — removes a hub-installed skill.
- `hermes skills opt-out [--remove]` — writes `.no-bundled-skills` marker to the active profile to stop future `hermes update` re-seeding.
- `hermes skills opt-in [--sync]` — removes the opt-out marker.
- `hermes skills reset <name> [--restore]` — clears user-modified tracking for a bundled skill.
- `hermes skills snapshot export|import` — bulk skill-config export/import.

There is no `hermes skill enable <name>` / `hermes skill disable <name>` verb. Flipping a skill's state is done by editing `config.yaml` `skills.disabled` (or via the `hermes skills config` TUI). Each profile owns its own copy, so the state is implicitly per-profile because `hermes -p <name> skills list` reads that profile's `HERMES_HOME/config.yaml`.
auditApproach:
Script #2 (Task C) should audit + flip skills by working through the Python API in `hermes_cli.profiles` and `agent.skill_utils` (or the same file paths they use), never by spawning `hermes` subshells. Recommended strategy:

AUDIT (read-only, walk every profile):
1. Resolve all profiles via `hermes_cli.profiles.list_profiles()` -> List[ProfileInfo]. Returns default + all named profiles, with name / path / is_default / skill_count / distribution metadata / description. This already does `_count_skills()` per profile and reads the per-profile `profile.yaml` description.
2. For each profile, read `<profile_dir>/config.yaml` (via `hermes_cli.config.load_config(path=...)`, NOT the global load_config) and call `get_disabled_skills(config)` to extract the global disabled list; for per-platform state, call `get_disabled_skills(config, platform)` for each entry in `PLATFORMS`.
3. For each profile, walk `<profile_dir>/skills/**/*.md` (use `agent.skill_utils.is_excluded_skill_path` to filter excluded paths) and parse `SKILL.md` YAML frontmatter to get `name`, `description`, `category`. Cross-reference with the disabled list.
4. Use `tools.skills_sync._read_manifest()` to identify bundled skills, and `tools.skills_hub.HubLockFile().list_installed()` to identify hub-installed skills (so the audit report can tag each skill as bundled/hub/local and enabled/disabled).
5. Build a structured `AuditReport` dataclass: `{profile_name, profile_path, skills: [{name, category, source, enabled, description}]}`. Render as bilingual EN+HU table + JSON sidecar for downstream tools.

FLIP (idempotent state edits, prefer Python API over raw file edits):
1. For each profile dir, load its config with `hermes_cli.config.load_config(path=str(profile_dir / "config.yaml"))`.
2. Compute the new `disabled` set: `new_disabled = (current_disabled - to_enable) | to_disable`.
3. Persist with `hermes_cli.config.save_config(config, path=...)` (the in-repo `save_config` is path-aware when called with `path=`). This writes back to the per-profile `config.yaml` atomically (write-tmp + rename). Mirror for `platform_disabled[<platform>]` if the caller wants per-platform state.
4. After the edit, invalidate the prompt-builder cache for that profile via `agent.prompt_builder.clear_skills_system_prompt_cache(clear_snapshot=True)` so the next chat session picks up the new state. The cache file is `~/.hermes/.skills_prompt_snapshot.json`.
5. To install the migrated skill-creator, use `hermes_cli.skills_hub.do_install(identifier, name_override=..., skip_confirm=True, force=True, invalidate_cache=True)`. Pass `HERMES_HOME=<profile_dir>` in the subprocess env (do_install internally uses `get_hermes_home()` which reads the env var, so direct in-process call already respects it).
6. To uninstall an existing skill by name, use `hermes_cli.skills_hub.do_uninstall(name)` or remove the directory directly: `shutil.rmtree(<profile_dir>/skills/<category>/<name>)` + remove from hub lockfile.

DO NOT use raw YAML edits to `config.yaml` when the API path is available — it bypasses the `cfg_get` platform-resolution indirection and the atomic-write safety in `save_config`. The skill resolution pipeline in `agent.skill_utils` is the single source of truth; mirroring its logic in Script #2 is exactly what the `get_disabled_skill_names` and `get_disabled_skills` functions exist to make safe.

The audit run should be idempotent and re-runnable: produce a deterministic JSON report keyed by `(profile_name, skill_name)` so flips can be diffed across runs. The flip phase should print bilingual EN+HU diffs and require an `--apply` flag by default for safety.
relevantCliCommands: hermes profile list # list all profiles (default + named)
hermes profile show <name> # show profile details
hermes profile use <name> # set sticky default (writes ~/.hermes/active_profile)
hermes -p <name> <any-cmd> # override HERMES_HOME for one invocation

hermes profile create <name> [--clone|--clone-all|--clone-from <src>] [--no-alias] [--no-skills] [--description "..."] # create new profile (auto-seeds bundled skills unless --no-skills; creates wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name>)
hermes profile delete <name> [-y] # remove profile dir + wrapper + service
hermes profile rename <old> <new> # rename + migrate honcho host + update wrapper
hermes profile describe <name> [--text "..."|--auto] # edit profile.yaml description
hermes profile alias <name> [--name <custom>] [--remove] # manage wrapper script
hermes profile export <name> [-o out.tar.gz] # portable archive (excludes auth.json + .env)
hermes profile import <archive> [--name <name>] # restore from archive
hermes profile install <git-url-or-dir> [--name <name>] [--alias] [--force] [-y] # install a distribution (writes distribution.yaml)
hermes profile update <name> [--force-config] [-y] # re-pull distribution
hermes profile info <name> # show distribution manifest
hermes skills list [--source all|hub|builtin|local] [--enabled-only] # list with enabled/disabled status
hermes skills config # interactive curses UI to toggle individual skills or categories globally / per-platform
hermes skills install <identifier> [--category <cat>] [--name <name>] [--force] [-y] # install from skills.sh / GitHub / URL
hermes skills uninstall <name> # remove a hub skill
hermes skills opt-out [--remove] [-y] # write .no-bundled-skills marker + optionally delete pristine bundled skills
hermes skills opt-in [--sync] # remove marker; --sync re-seeds now
hermes skills reset <name> [--restore] [-y] # clear user-modified tracking
hermes skills check [name] / skills update [name] / skills audit [name] [--deep] # hub maintenance
hermes skills snapshot export <file> | import <file> # bulk skill config
hermes skills tap list|add <owner/repo>|remove <name> # manage skill sources

relevantSourceFiles.role: Canonical profile-management module. Defines ProfileInfo, validate/normalize rules, list_profiles, create_profile, delete_profile, rename_profile, export_profile, import_profile, get_active_profile/set_active_profile, resolve_profile_env, profile.yaml read/write, _get_default_hermes_home, _get_active_profile_path, _get_profiles_root, create_wrapper_script/remove_wrapper_script, seed_profile_skills, has_bundled_skills_opt_out. THIS is the primary API Script #2 should call.
Argparse parser for `hermes profile <subcmd>` (list, use, create, delete, describe, show, alias, rename, export, import, install, update, info). Wires cmd_profile in main.py as the handler.

Holds cmd_profile (lines 10003+) which dispatches each profile_action to profiles.py functions. Also contains _apply_profile_override() (lines 325-510) which pre-parses -p/--profile from argv BEFORE any module import, then sets HERMES_HOME; plus HERMES_S6_SUPERVISED_CHILD special case.
Defines get_hermes_home() (env-var resolution with active_profile fallback) and get_default_hermes_root() (the profile-anchor root, used to locate the profiles/ dir and active_profile file).

Skills enable/disable TUI. get_disabled_skills(config, platform) and save_disabled_skills(config, disabled, platform) are the per-platform-aware mutators that write config["skills"]["disabled"] and config["skills"]["platform_disabled"]. skills_command() is the curses UI entry.

Runtime source of truth for disabled-skill resolution. get_disabled_skill_names(platform) reads config.yaml directly (no CLI dependency) and unions global+platform lists. Also defines is_excluded_skill_path (used during skill scans) and _load_raw_config (cached YAML loader).

Skills hub CLI. do_install(identifier, ...) (line 478) for installing via skills.sh / GitHub / URL; do_uninstall, do_opt_out, do_opt_in, do_list(enabled_only=...), skills_command(args) (line 1595) dispatches the argparse subactions. Also exposes do_audit, do_check, do_update, do_reset, do_publish, do_snapshot, do_tap.
Argparse parser for `hermes skills <subcmd>` (browse, search, install, inspect, list, check, update, audit, uninstall, reset, opt-out, opt-in, repair-official, publish, snapshot, tap, config).

load_config(path=...) and save_config(config, path=...) are the per-config.yaml mutators. Script #2 should call save_config(config, path=str(profile_dir/"config.yaml")) to persist disabled-list edits atomically. cfg_get(skills_cfg, "platform_disabled", platform) resolves the per-platform key.
LLM-driven description auto-generation (used by `hermes profile describe --auto`). Read-only reference for how to iterate profile skills.
Distribution install/update logic for `hermes profile install/update` (git URL or local dir with distribution.yaml). Read-only reference for profile lifecycle.
clear_skills_system_prompt_cache(clear_snapshot=True) must be called after flipping skills so the next session reloads; the cache file is ~/.hermes/.skills_prompt_snapshot.json.
sync_skills() and _read_manifest() identify bundled skills (manifest at ~/.hermes/skills/.bundled_manifest). set_bundled_skills_opt_out/remove_pristine_bundled_skills support the opt-out flow.
Hub lockfile (HubLockFile) and source router (create_source_router) used by do_install. The installed-skills lockfile is at <HERMES_HOME>/skills/.hub_lock.json.

Per-profile gateway service registration (systemd / launchd / s6). _profile_dir_for_gateway_service() derives the gateway service suffix. Profiles auto-register a gateway s6 service in container mode.
_profile_suffix(), _profile_arg(), _gateway_run_args_for_profile() — helpers for the per-profile gateway lifecycle.
Live example of the per-profile metadata file (description + description_auto). Read by profiles.read_profile_meta().
Human-readable profile README (separate from profile.yaml; not read by Hermes code).
Live example of an installed skill directory (the one Script #1 patches + Script #2 enables after migration). Currently lives at <HERMES_HOME>/skills/<category>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md.

evidence:
1) PROFILE STORAGE. profiles.py docstring (lines 1-20) declares: "Each profile is a fully independent HERMES_HOME directory... Profiles live under ``~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/`` by default. The 'default' profile is ``~/.hermes`` itself." Live evidence: `ls /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/profiles/` shows 17 named profiles (architect, backend-coder, code-reviewer, ..., ux-designer) plus the default root. The per-profile structure is enforced in profiles._PROFILE_DIRS (lines 39-53) listing the subdirs every profile bootstraps.

2) profile.yaml format. profiles.read_profile_meta() (lines 654-676) and write_profile_meta() (lines 679-709) read/write `{description: str, description_auto: bool}`. Sample: `cat /Users/kiscsicska/.hermes/profiles/architect/profile.yaml` returns `description: Designs system architecture...` / `description_auto: false`. Comment at lines 636-647 explains why profile.yaml is separate from config.yaml.

3) active_profile sticky file. profiles._get_active_profile_path() (line 269) returns `_get_default_hermes_home() / "active_profile"`. get_active_profile() (lines 1399-1411) reads it, returns "default" if missing/empty. set_active_profile() (lines 1414-1436) writes atomically; removes the file when "default" is selected.

4) DEFAULT PROFILE RESOLUTION. main.py _apply_profile_override() (lines 325-510) is the documented canonical resolution. Order: explicit -p/--profile flag -> HERMES_HOME env (only if parent dir is "profiles") -> ~/.hermes/active_profile -> platform default (~/.hermes). Comment at lines 334 calls out "Falls back to ~/.hermes/active_profile for sticky default." Line 467: `if profile_name is None and not os.environ.get("HERMES_S6_SUPERVISED_CHILD"):` shows the S6 gateway-slot exception. resolve_profile_env() in profiles.py (lines 1801-1817) is the name->HERMES_HOME-path resolver.

5) SKILL ENABLE/DISABLE MECHANISM. skills_config.py docstring (lines 5-12) declares the storage shape: `skills: { disabled: [...], platform_disabled: { telegram: [...] } }`. get_disabled_skills() (lines 27-42) returns the global set unioned with the platform set; save_disabled_skills() (lines 45-53) writes back via save_config. agent/skill_utils.get_disabled_skill_names() (lines 318-353) is the runtime resolver that does NOT import the CLI module and reads config.yaml directly. skills_hub.do_list(enabled_only=True) (lines 884-979) demonstrates the integration: it reads get_disabled_skill_names() and annotates each skill with enabled/disabled. There is no `hermes skill enable/disable` command — the only mutator is `hermes skills config` (interactive TUI) or direct config.yaml edits.

6) CLI COMMANDS. `hermes profile <subcmd>` is registered in subcommands/profile.py (build_profile_parser, lines 12-203) listing every subaction including list, use, create, delete, describe, show, alias, rename, export, import, install, update, info. `hermes skills <subcmd>` is in subcommands/skills.py (build_skills_parser, lines 12-269) listing browse, search, install, inspect, list, check, update, audit, uninstall, reset, opt-out, opt-in, repair-official, publish, snapshot, tap, config. Dispatcher: main.py:10003 cmd_profile and main.py:11396 cmd_skills. Sub-dispatchers: hermes_cli/profiles.py + hermes_cli/skills_hub.py:1595 skills_command.

7) WRAPPER ALIASES. profiles.create_wrapper_script (lines 394-431) writes a POSIX `#!/bin/sh\nexec <hermes> -p <id> "$@"\n` to ~/.local/bin/<id> (or .bat on Windows). The bare `coder` command works because the wrapper execs `hermes -p coder "$@"`, which goes back through _apply_profile_override().

8) NO_BUNDLED_SKILLS_MARKER. profiles.NO_BUNDLED_SKILLS_MARKER = ".no-bundled-skills" (line 133). has_bundled_skills_opt_out() (lines 136-141) checks for it. seed_profile_skills() (lines 976-1018) and skills_hub.do_opt_out/do_opt_in manage it.
